zh.lifeSummary: Sir Thomas de Ros是William de Ros of Presson and Kendal和Elizabeth的兒子，於大約1307年出生在英國維斯特莫藍德。他和de Preston至少有3個兒子和1個女兒。他去世於1390年11月20日在英國英格蘭維斯特莫藍德肯達爾享年84歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Ros
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Enrique, Luis, Jose, Alberto, Eduardo, Rafael, Elena, Fidel, Joaquin, Manuel, Pablo, Ramon.  Southeast Asian Leang, Phon, Lap, Phan, Pho, Saroeun, Sophal, Sophan, Sophea, Tha, Thang, Yen.
Catalan: nickname for someone with blond hair or a fair complexion, ultimately from Latin russus.

variants.name: Rus
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Romanian Simion, Grigore, Doina, Dorel, Ilie, Liviu, Petru, Vasile.

Czech, Slovak, and Romanian (of Slavic origin): ethnic name for a Russian, ultimately derived from an Old Norse term meaning ‘oarsman, rower’ (from the root roths-), a reference to the fact that the Russian state was established by Scandinavians who rowed their ships up rivers inland from the Baltic sea. In some cases this may also be origin of the Slovenian and Croatian surname (see 3 below).
Czech: descriptive nickname from the adjective rusý ‘blond’.

variants.name: Ross
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from one or other of a number of Scottish and English places called Ross or Roos(e), especially Roose (Lancashire) and Roos (East Yorkshire). The placenames derive from a British ancestor of Welsh rhos ‘moor, heath, plain’, which is the sense of the cognate Gaelic word ros. The Scottish surname has at least three origins. First, the Anglo-Norman family from Roos (East Yorkshire) was introduced to Scotland when Robert of Roos, lord of Wark Castle (Northumberland), married Isabella, an illegitimate daughter of King William the Lion. Second, various families took the name from the province of Ross in northern Scotland and other places of that name. Third, there were Norman families in Scotland by the 13th century who probably derived their name from Rots in Normandy (see 2 below). The descendants of Godfrey de Ros, tenant of the de Morville lords of Cunninghame, were major landholders in Ayrshire, and almost certainly took their name from Rots. The Rose family of Kilravock (Nairnshire) may take their name from either of these three (see Rose ). The lairds of Balnagown adopted the surname Ross after the earldom of Ross (to which they considered themselves rightful heirs) had passed into other hands through the female line.

English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Rots in Calvados (France), probably named with the ancient Germanic element rod ‘clearing’ (compare Rhodes ). This was the original home of a family de Ros, who were established in Kent in 1130 and had major estates in Kent until well into the 13th century.

English: habitational name from Wrose, in Shipley, near Bradford (Yorkshire), with re-spelling of Wr- as R- due to the loss of /w/ before /r/ in early modern English pronunciation. The spelling Wrose is no longer current. The placename derives from Old English wrāse ‘knot, something twisted’, referring to the steep-sided hill on which the settlement stands, with the sense ‘broken or twisting hill’.

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: Sir Thomas de Ros was born about 1307, in Westmorland, England as the son of William de Ros of Presson and Kendal and Elizabeth. He had at least 3 sons and 1 daughter with de Preston. He died on 20 November 1390, in Kendal, Westmorland, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 84.

fr.lifeSummary: Sir Thomas de Ros est né environ 1307, à Westmorland, Angleterre, fils de William de Ros of Presson and Kendal et Elizabeth. Il a eu au moins 3 fils et 1 fille avec de Preston. Il est décédé le 20 novembre 1390, à Kendal, Westmorland, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 84 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Sir Thomas de Ros wurde um 1307 in Westmorland, England, als Sohn von William de Ros of Presson and Kendal und Elizabeth geboren. Er hatte mit de Preston mindestens 3 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb am 20. November 1390 im Alter von 84 Jahren in Kendal, Westmorland, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Sir Thomas de Ros è nato il circa 1307 nel Westmorland, England come figlio di William de Ros of Presson and Kendal e Elizabeth. Lui avuto almeno 3 figli e 1 figlia con de Preston. Morì il 20 novembre 1390, nel Kendal, Westmorland, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 84 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Thomas de Rosは約1307年にイギリスウエストモーランドでWilliam de Ros of Presson and KendalとElizabethの息子として生まれた。彼はde Prestonとの間に少なくとも3人の息子と1人の娘をもうけた。彼は1390年11月20日にイギリスイギリスウエストモーランドケンダルで84歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Thomas de Ros(은)는 대략 1307년 영국 웨스트모를랜드에서 William de Ros of Presson and Kendal(와)과 Elizabeth의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 de Preston(와)과 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1390년 11월 20일 영국 잉글랜드 웨스트모를랜드 켄다에서 84세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Sir Thomas de Ros nasceu aproximadamente 1307, em Westmorland, Inglaterra filho de William de Ros of Presson and Kendal e Elizabeth. Ele teve pelo menos 3 filhos e 1 filha com de Preston. Ele faleceu em 20 de novembro de 1390, em Kendal, Westmorland, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 84 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Sir Thomas de Ros родился около 1307 г. в местности вестморланд, Англия. Его родителей звали William de Ros of Presson and Kendal и Elizabeth. У него было по крайней мере 3 сына и 1 дочь от de Preston. Он умер 20 ноября 1390 г. в местности кендаль, вестморланд, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 84 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Sir Thomas de Ros nació aproximadamente 1307, en Westmorland, Inglaterra como hijo de William de Ros of Presson and Kendal y Elizabeth. Tuvo por lo menos 3 hijos y 1 hija con de Preston. Murió el 20 de noviembre de 1390, en Kendal, Westmorland, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 84 años.
